message:
feat(scanner): graduated confidence for structural rescue + .rch-target- pattern

Improve classification accuracy for cargo target directory detection:

- Structural rescue now uses graduated confidence: 0.75 when 3+ cargo
  markers (.fingerprint, deps, incremental, build) are present vs 0.55
  for fewer markers — a directory with most markers is definitively a
  cargo target and deserves higher confidence
- Add new ArtifactPattern for `.rch-target-*` directories (dot-prefixed,
  hyphenated variant) at 0.92 confidence, closing a coverage gap for
  the rch remote compilation tool's naming convention
- Update extract_pattern_label to also match `.rch-target-` prefix
- Add 6 new unit tests covering few-marker rescue, many-marker rescue,
  all-marker rescue, no-marker non-rescue, and .rch-target- detection
- Cargo.lock: ftui family crates bumped 0.2.1 → 0.3.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
